content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Both amendments identify legitimate hardening gaps consistent with this branch's purpose of protecting create-from-bead and same-bead project selection. fr-10-3407b7e1 correctly flags that active_same_bead_conflict_error only verifies the referenced project exists, not that its task_source actually points back at the same milestone/bead; a corrupt task-runs.ndjson row or a manual same-id project would send the operator to an unrelated project instead of surfacing an orphaned-lineage repair error, and milestone_bead_refs_match is already the canonical comparator used elsewhere. fr-10-e56d1fc0 correctly flags that both reject_existing_same_bead_project_creation and find_existing_bead_project silently collapse multiple same-bead candidates to a single arbitrary winner; because find_existing_bead_project feeds ensure_project_for_controller (which then adopts and runs the chosen project), the silent selection can mask the exact ambiguous duplicate state the branch is hardening against, and failing closed with a repair error is the consistent policy.

## Votes

- `fr-10-3407b7e1`: Accept
  active_same_bead_conflict_error currently only verifies read_project_record succeeds (service.rs:2649-2661) and derives DuplicateActiveBead / retry-resume guidance from run.json status (lines 2682-2717) without ever checking project.toml's task_source. A corrupt or hand-edited task-runs row pointing at an unrelated project, or a project without matching task_source, would then produce guidance sending the operator into the wrong project instead of the repair-style RunStartFailed path. Validating task_source.milestone_id and task_source.bead_id with milestone_bead_refs_match (already used at fs.rs:4418,4610,4766,5193,5422 and project.rs:740) and returning the same active_lineage_repair_error on mismatch is consistent with the existing ProjectNotFound and CorruptRecord branches and directly matches the branch's hardening goal.
- `fr-10-e56d1fc0`: Accept
  reject_existing_same_bead_project_creation (project.rs:662-704) and find_existing_bead_project (project.rs:782-796) sort matching projects by status/default/lexical order and take the first. For reject_existing_same_bead_project_creation this produces a block referencing only one of several competing projects, masking co-existing duplicates the operator needs to repair. More importantly, find_existing_bead_project is consumed by ensure_project_for_controller (milestone.rs:1146) which auto-adopts the returned project via sync_controller_task_claimed, so two non-terminal same-bead projects silently cause milestone run to commit to one arbitrary project and ignore the other. Because this branch exists specifically to harden against duplicate same-bead project state, failing closed with an ambiguity error listing the competing project ids is the right policy; the existing intentional preference (non-terminal retry over completed default, covered by the retry-over-completed test) can be preserved as the single-unambiguous-candidate case.
